- Liest Aläu Stotem ammelisen Geschicht.
- Ech sinn 1962 zu Zeissingen op d'Welt komm.
- Ech sinn och do an d' Schoul gaangen.
- Mat 20 Joer sinn ech selwer Léierin an der Zeissinger Primärschoul ginn.
- Ech wollt joeng Gesellin bleiwen, mee du hunn ech de Fernokäne geléiert an ech hunn mech verléift.
- Wéi ech 25 Joer hat, hu mir eis bestuet an ech hunn opgehalen mat schaffen.
- Mir sinn op Bettelbréck geplënnert an ee Joer méi spéit hu mir eis éischt Kand kritt, d'Leonie.
- Dat war eng ganz flott Zäite, do hu mir och eisen éischten Auto kaaft.
- Dat war 1961, wann ech mech richteg erënneren.
- 1992, also wéi ech 30 Joer hat, koum eis zweet Kand, de François, op d'Welt.
- Zéng Joer méi spéit hu mir eis éischt Rees mam Fliger gemaach.
- Mir sinn a Spuenje geflunn, dat war eng richteg Avantür.
- Du no hu mir nach méi dacks am Fliger, dat ass ee Joer eng Normalitéit ginn.
- Wéi ech 50 Joer hat, verend konn a scho grouss an ech hat nëmmen vill Zäite.
- Ech hunn du ugefaangen, Nohilfskore fir Kanner ze ginn.
- Elo fille ech mech awer ze al dofir an ech genéissen d'Pensioun zesumme mat mengem Mann.
- Mir reese nare ma géer an nächst Joer wëlle mir d'Leonie besiche fueren.
- Hat wunnt de loo mat sengem Mann an Amerika zu Boston.
- Read Aläu Stotem's collections history.
- I was born in 1962 in Zeissingen to the world.
- I also went to school there.
- At 20 I myself became a teacher at the Zeissinger primary school.
- I wanted to stay a young companion, but you learned the Fernokäne and fell in love.
- When I was 25 we got married and I stopped working.
- We planned to move to Bettelbréck and a year later we had our first child, Leonie.
- That was a very nice time, we also bought our first car then.
- It was 1961, if I remember correctly.
- In 1992, when I was 30, our second child, François, was born.
- Ten years later we took our first trip by plane.
- We flew to Spain, that was a real adventure.
- After that we flew even more often, that became a normal thing in a year.
- When I was 50 I could have died and was already grown and I had only many times.
- I started to give little helpers for children.
- Now I feel too old for that and I enjoy retirement together with my husband.
- Next year we will travel to Leonie again to visit.
- He lived the man with his husband in America in Boston.

- Firwat sidd Dir op Lëtzebuerg komm?
- Ech sinn op Lëtzebuerg komm, well meng Fra hei eng Aarbecht kritt huet.
- Wéi huet Iech Lëtzebuerg gefall?
- Mir huet et direkt immens gutt gefall. Kleng, mee ganz villseiteg.
- Hutt Dir Iech séier ageliewt?
- Ech hunn mech och ganz séier ageliewt a vill Leit kennegeléiert.
- Why are you in Luxembourg?
- I am in Luxembourg because my wife got a job here.
- How did you like Luxembourg?
- I liked it right away a lot. Small, but very diverse.
- Did you settle in quickly?
- I settled in quickly as well and met a lot of people.

- Ech wollt am Fong an Däitschland studéiere goen,

- vun der Multikulturalitéit hei.
- An elo sinn ech frou,

- I wanted to study in Germany at the beginning,
- but there was the shortage of study spots.
- I was immensely disappointed.
- I had also enrolled at the University of Luxembourg,

- I was fascinated by the world,

- And now I am happy,
- that I am here.

- I missed my friends in Italy, but it was also nice to meet new people from all over the world.
- And my teachers at school were still very helpful.

- Läustert den Ekton dilio a kontrolléiert.
- E süfe jo fönnef Joar mat meiner Familie auf Letzebursch gepläntert.
- Wéi mir hei ukomm sinn, hatte mir nach keng Wuning an hun drei Wochen an engem Apart-Hotel gewoond.
- Eis Kanner waren nach klang, demols hatte sie drei a fönnef Joar.
- Eglik war Summer an mir konnten viel erausgoen.
- Den Hotel war direkt bei engem klange Park mit enger flotter Spillplatz.
- Moies um mir Kaffee gedronk, an dann sind mir auf die Spillplatz gangen.
- Am Ufank hun Kanner immer allein gespielt.
- Mir dun hun sie sehr e pur klang Spielkommode fond.
- Mir Kanner hun richtig Italienisch geschwart, mir für sie an hier Neu frönn war dat kee Problem.
- Enges Dags, so nur zwei Wochen, war ein Frau mit einem klangen Jong von ungefähr vier Joar op der Spillplatz.
- Öch hat sie vierdrun nach nie do gesehn.
- Hire Jong hat eleng am Sand gespilt, hier nur zuschnitt für die aner Kanner interessiert.
- Ob eemol huet hien ugefang ganz hart zu sungen, en italienisch Kannerlied.
- Mir Kanner hun verdächt komisch geguckt, an dun hu sie hart matgesungen.
- Sie hun dat Lied natürlich kannt.
- A so sind sie Mama nisch an Gespräch komm.
- A wat witzisch war, sie ware go net aus Italien, mir aus Brasilien.
- Die Kleingen hat dat Lied aus einer internationalen Kriesch geleiert.
- Zu guter Lescht hun Kanner allmatt nie gespilt, an sie noch haut nach frönn.
- Listen to the Ekton control it.
- A sweetie yes five years with my family in Luxembourg joked.
- When we arrived here, I did not have a home yet and had lived in an apartment hotel for three weeks.
- Our children were still small, sometimes they were three and five years old.
- Often it was summer and we could go out a lot.
- The hotel was right by a small park with a lively playground.
- In the morning we drank coffee, and then we went to the playground.
- At first the children always played alone.
- We then had them a very a bit boring game console.
- Our children spoke real Italian, for them and here new friends was not a problem.
- In one day, just two weeks, there was a woman with a small boy of about four years on the playground.
- I then four times did not see him there again.
- Her boy played eagerly in the sand, just a bit for the other children interested.
- At one point he started very loudly singing an Italian children's song.
- Our children looked strangely at him, and then they sang along with him.
- They knew the song, of course.
- And so the mom was not in the conversation.
- And what was funny, they were not from Italy, we from Brazil.
- The kids learned the song from an international school.
- Finally the children did not play at all and they still want to sing.

- Liest a lauscht dat Textofschnitt.
- Aus Indien op Letzebuerg.
- De Kumar ass an Indien gebuer an opgewues.
- Hien huet sech schonn als Kand fir Medizin interesséiert.
- An dofir ass hien op Duni gaangen, fir Dokter ze ginn.
- Noem Studium huet hie seng eegen Praxis zu New Delhi opgemaach.
- An als Generalist geschafft.
- Den Ennerscheed tëscht Ärm a Räich ass grouss an Indien.
- An de Kumar huet sech sozial staark engagéiert.
- An am iehme Kachiefen der Staat seng medizinescht Hëllef ugebueden.
- Op engem Kongress zu London huet de Kumar eng Lëtzebuergerin kennengeléiert.
- Hien ass sech verléift.
- No zwee Joer Fernbezéiung hunn de Elisabeth an de Kumar sech an Indien an och zu Lëtzebuerg bestuet.
- An decidéiert, sech zu Lëtzebuerg néierzelassen.
- Fir sech zesummen hiert Liewen opzebauen an eng Famill ze grënnen.
- De Migratioun op Lëtzebuerg war net einfach fir de Kumar.
- Well hie seng Famill an Indien verluer huet.
- A seng Praxis huet missen opginn.
- Zu Lëtzebuerg konnt hien net méi als Dokter schaffen.
- De Kumar huet Hindi, Urdu an Englesch geschwat, wéi hien op Lëtzebuerg komm ass.
- Fir um Aarbechtsmarché eng Chance ze hunn, huet hien intensiv Franséisch a Lëtzebuergesch geléiert.
- De leschte Joer huet de Kumar als Interim a verschiddene Laboratoiren a Büroen geschafft.
- Duerch Kollegen ass hien dann an Kontakt komm mat enger Organisatioun, déi medizinesch Hëllef a Krisengebitter liwwert.
- Mat senger berufflecher Erfahrung war de Kumar e Gewënner fir d’Organisatioun.
- Sënnerher ass hien oft am Ausland.
- A wount hei zu Lëtzebuerg fir si aktiv.
- A këmmert sech ëm Impfprojeten an anere Weltlänner.
- De Kumar huet de Kontakt mat sengem heemechten Erwuess verluer.
- No zwee Joer flitt hien mat senger Fra an de Kanner op New Delhi, fir d’Famill ze besichen.
- Hien huet och eng Fondatioun zu Lëtzebuerg gegrënnt.
- Fir Spenden fir Spriecherkanner an Indien ze sammelen.
- Read and listen to that transcription.
- From India to Luxembourg.
- Kumar was born in India and grew up there.
- He showed an interest in medicine as a child.
- Therefore he went to Duni to become a doctor.
- After studies he opened his own practice in New Delhi.
- And worked as a general practitioner.
- The difference between poor and rich is large in India.
- And Kumar became socially engaged.
- And at his own expense he offered medical aid to the state in need.
- At a congress in London Kumar met a Luxembourgish woman.
- He fell in love.
- After two years of a long-distance relationship, Elisabeth and Kumar married in India and also in Luxembourg.
- And decided to settle in Luxembourg.
- To build a life together and start a family.
- Migration to Luxembourg was not easy for Kumar.
- Because he lost his family in India.
- And he had to give up his practice.
- In Luxembourg he could no longer work as a doctor.
- Kumar spoke Hindi, Urdu and English when he came to Luxembourg.
- To have a chance on the labor market, he learned intensive French and Luxembourgish.
- In the last year Kumar worked as interim in several laboratories and offices.
- Through colleagues he came into contact with an organization that provides medical aid in crisis areas.
- With his professional experience Kumar was a winner for the organization.
- He travels a lot.
- And lives here in Luxembourg for her active.
- And he takes care of vaccination projects and other countries around the world.
- Kumar lost contact with his homegrown upbringing.
- After two years he flies with his wife and children to New Delhi to visit the family.
- He also founded a foundation in Luxembourg.
- To raise donations for scholarship children in India.

- From India to Luxembourg.
- Kumar was born in India and grew up there.
- He showed an interest in medicine as a child.
- Therefore he went to Duni to become a doctor.
- After studies he opened his own practice in New Delhi.
- And worked as a general practitioner.
- The difference between poor and rich is large in India.
- And Kumar became socially engaged.
- And at his own expense he offered medical aid to the state in need.
- At a congress in London Kumar met a Luxembourgish woman.
- He fell in love.
- After two years of a long-distance relationship, Elisabeth and Kumar married in India and also in Luxembourg.
- And decided to settle in Luxembourg.
- To build a life together and start a family.
- Migration to Luxembourg was not easy for Kumar.
- Because he lost his family in India.
- And he had to give up his practice.
- In Luxembourg he could no longer work as a doctor.
- Kumar spoke Hindi, Urdu and English when he came to Luxembourg.
- To have a chance on the labor market, he learned intensive French and Luxembourgish.
- In the last year Kumar worked as interim in several laboratories and offices.
- Through colleagues he came into contact with an organization that provides medical aid in crisis areas.
- With his professional experience Kumar was a winner for the organization.
- He travels a lot.
- And lives here in Luxembourg for her active.
- And he takes care of vaccination projects and other countries around the world.
- Kumar lost contact with his homegrown upbringing.
- After two years he flies with his wife and children to New Delhi to visit the family.
- He also founded a foundation in Luxembourg.
- To raise donations for scholarship children in India.

- 1828 huet awer den deemolische Keeser vu Brasilien, den Dom Pedro den Eichten, decidéiert, datt keng europäesch Immigrante méi dierften a Brasilien kommen.
- Zu der Zäit ware Gruppen vu ongeféier honnert Letzebuerger en awee fir dohin.
- Si haten zu Letzebuerch alles verkaf, fir a Brasilien en neit Liewen unzefänken, mee wéi si zu Bremen geschëffelt hunn, huet dat net méi gaangen.
- Si hunn erëm misse zeréck op Letzebuerch kommen an hat de Gwen Eicht méi.
- D'Letzebuerger Regierung huet hinnen awer zum Deel gehollef, a si kruten eng Plaz, wou si sech konnten néierloossen.
- Sou ass op der Grenz vun de Gemenge Gréissbous, Wal an Heischent, e Neidorf entstanen, dat deemols 1828 nei Brasilien geheescht huet.
- Haut heescht et Gréiwels a gehéiert zu der Gemeng Wal.
- De Guy Helminger, e letzebuerger Schrëftsteller, huet den däitschsproocheche Roman "Nei Brasilien" geschriwwen, deen d'Geschicht vun Immigranten op Letzebuerch an Immigranten, déi schlussendlech a nei Brasilie gelieft hunn, erzielt.
- Mee den Efa kneppt.
- At the beginning of the 19th century, many people in Brazil still emigrated.
- However, in 1828 the then-emperor of Brazil, Dom Pedro the Second, decided that no European immigrants may come to Brazil anymore.
- At that time groups of about a hundred Luxembourgers were on their way there.
- They had sold everything in Luxembourg to start a new life in Brazil, but when they sailed to Bremen, that no longer worked.
- They had to return to Luxembourg, and they had even less.
- The Luxembourg government helped them in part, and they got a place where they could settle.
- Thus, on the border of the municipalities Grëissbous, Wal, and Heischent, a new village arose, which at the time was called Neider- or New Brazil in 1828.
- Today it is called Gréiwels and belongs to the municipality Wal.
- Guy Helminger, a Luxembourgish writer, wrote the German-language novel "Nei Brasilien," which tells the history of immigrants in Luxembourg and immigrants who eventually lived in New Brazil.
- But the Efa kicks.
